items.title: After initial backlash, how do locals feel about the 'Blue Blob' now?

children.content: Cast in Blue has been the subject of criticism, ridicule and even vandalism over the past 10 months, but Mount Gambier's community has begun to embrace it.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing a black and white top and glasses stands next to a blue sculpture with her arm wrapped around its trunk.

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing a black and white top and glasses stands next to a blue sculpture with her arm wrapped around its trunk.

items.title: Magnitude-5.5 earthquake 'morning wake-up call' to remote community

children.content: A remote community in the APY Lands have woken up to rattling shelves and swinging lights caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake, Geoscience Australia says.

image.altText: A red dirt football oval next to a grassed football oval and a small community with a mountain range in the background

items.title: Fuel prices force commute changes, as food becomes 'privilege' for some

children.content: A growing number of Australians are factoring rising fuel costs into a tightening budget, including seeking cheaper alternative transport, with one charity warning the flow on effects of the crisis are causing household stress for many families.

image.altText: A woman looks serious, leans an arm on the bonnet of a SUV parked in a driveway

items.title: MVP starts hot in winner-takes-all game five of NBL Finals against Kings

children.content: The NBL Finals between the Sydney Kings and Adelaide 36ers come down to game five after a heated series. Follow live.

image.altText: Adelaide 36ers' Bryce Cotton shoots over Xavier Cooks of the Sydney Kings in the NBL Finals.

items.title: Sick of not knowing if your avocado is ripe or bruised? This scanner could change that

children.content: An Australian-first avocado "ripeness scanner" is being trialled, with the aim of telling customers exactly when their avocados will be ready to eat.

image.altText: A close up of green avocados inside a white crate.

items.title: 'So few precedents': One Nation leaves SA Liberals clinging to opposition status

children.content: The Liberal Party will have just one more seat than One Nation in South Australia’s lower house, leaving opposition leader Ashton Hurn little margin for error.

articles.title: How an Australian Sex Party candidate became a One Nation MP

children.content: Sixteen years before Jason Virgo won the SA seat of MacKillop for One Nation in the state's south-east, he twice ran for parliament as an Australian Sex Party candidate.

image.altText: Jason Virgo in a business shirt smiles and stands in front of other One Nation MPs

articles.title: One Nation wins last SA lower house seat following recount

children.content: The seat of Narungga was so close it required a recount, and has now been won by One Nation candidate Chantelle Thomas by a margin of 58 votes over the Liberals.

image.altText: Chantelle Thomas in a dark jumper looks at camera with a smile in a park

gallery.altText: composite image of Cory Bernardi outdoors and Ashton Hurn in a dark room, both looking serious

thumbnail.altText: composite image of Cory Bernardi outdoors and Ashton Hurn in a dark room, both looking serious

items.title: SA Electoral Commission apologises to staff for post-election pay delay

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has apologised to staff who have not been fully paid almost a fortnight after the state election, saying a payroll processing delay had caused the problem.

image.altText: An electoral officer at a polling station.

items.title: 'It's quite barren': SA fishers demand action to protect stocks

children.content: Easter is a popular time for consumers to purchase fish, but commercial fishers in South Australia say stocks have been decimated by the algal bloom. They fear it will get worse without further action.

image.altText: A South Australian commercial fisher in fishing clothes.

items.title: Cotton-Davis rivalry has gone global, 36ers coach says ahead of decider

children.content: As Sydney and Adelaide put the final touches on their preparations for this year's NBL championship decider, 36ers coach Mike Wells says he believes the rivalry his side has built with the Kings has attracted a global audience to the league.

gallery.altText: Mike Wells gestures from the sideline during Game 4 of the 2025/26 NBL championship series.

thumbnail.altText: Mike Wells gestures from the sideline during Game 4 of the 2025/26 NBL championship series.

items.title: Police look for missing cocaine after ocean 'drop-off' during 'bizarre' journey

children.content: Setting off from Panama just before Christmas, police say the MV Raider started its journey towards Sydney with almost 6 tonnes of cocaine aboard worth nearly $2 billion.

image.altText: A helicoper flies over a ship carrying drugs

items.title: Trash or treasure? Albanese's big gamble

children.content: Labor's landslide re-election brought renewed hope of reform, but in the past 12 months many anti-gambling advocates only became further disillusioned.

image.altText: Side on close up of Anthony Albanese, not speaking

items.title: Outback SA operator says Easter tourism 'worse than COVID', while others see spike

children.content: An Ikara-Flinders Ranges tourism operator says he is experiencing more cancellations than bookings this Easter as fuel shortage anxiety keep travellers away, while other regional towns expect to be booked out.

gallery.altText: A car drives into the sunset near the Flinders Ranges.

thumbnail.altText: A car drives into the sunset near the Flinders Ranges.

items.title: 'Doesn't make any sense': Young Aussies can't justify fuel costs to get to work

children.content: Young people are taking extraordinary steps to deal with the fuel crisis, including cutting back on work because the cost of fuel to get there is now higher than the money they will earn for the shift.

image.altText: A petrol station

items.title: Motorcyclist killed in traffic light crash in Adelaide, another rushed to hospital

children.content: A motorcyclist has died at Ovingham in Adelaide's inner north, hours after another rider was flown to hospital with life-threatening injuries sustained during a separate crash.

image.altText: An SA Police vehicle with flashing lights.

items.title: Australians find joy amid global uncertainty in Easter celebrations

children.content: Christians of all denominations gather at churches and cathedrals to mark Easter and reflect on their faith amid recent global turmoil.

articles.title: Aerial photographer's hunt for 'Easter eggs' yields stunning images

children.content: Scott McCook spends his spare time photographing the salt lakes of WA's Wheatbelt, which look uncannily like giant eggs when viewed from the air.

image.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt

gallery.altText: A service at St Pauls Cathedral

thumbnail.altText: A service at St Pauls Cathedral

items.title: Rowell returns as unbeaten Suns take on Demons

children.content: Gold Coast and Western Bulldogs look to maintain the last remaining 100 per cent records in the AFL, as they take on the Demons at the MCG and the Bombers at Docklands. Follow live.

image.altText: A Gold Coast Suns AFL player grimaces as he is tackled by a number of Melbourne players.

items.title: Cronulla halves dazzle as Sharks shine against Warriors

children.content: Nicho Hynes and Braydon Trindall have helped Cronulla to a healthy lead against an out of sorts Warriors side. Follow live.

image.altText: A rugby league player in blue holds the ball, tackled by players in white

items.title: Changing genders wasn't going to change how I felt about Kate

children.content: Kate could see her husband was miserable as a man and says she couldn't watch Marti be unhappy.

gallery.altText: Two women enjoying a cocktail and smiling for the camera
A man and woman on their wedding day looking very happy
Two women posing for a photo in front of a large banner of a cruise ship

items.title: Stagflation is already happening, a leading economist warns

children.content: With stagflation risks rising, what lessons could the treasurer learn from the past?

image.altText: An empty sign on petrol pumps

items.title: It hosted Kidman and Luhrmann, now famed regional cinema is up for sale

children.content: It's the cinema good enough for Luhrmann, Jackman and Kidman, and now this North Queensland icon is up for sale after 64 years.

image.altText: An elderly man in a blue tropical printed shirt standing in front of a movie theatre popcorn.

items.title: Queen at 100 is honoured in fashion exhibition as Andrew scandal bites

children.content: Within palace walls the Queen was a godlike figure. As her glorious fashion goes on display to commemorate what would have been her 100th birthday, how would she have responded to the Andrew crisis if alive today?

gallery.altText: Queen Elizabeth II in a long formal blue gown with bolero jacket and headpiece sitting on a red velvet seat with gold trim

thumbnail.altText: Queen Elizabeth II in a long formal blue gown with bolero jacket and headpiece sitting on a red velvet seat with gold trim

items.title: Lily's grandmother defied 50s' social norms to be both an artist and a mother

children.content: A new show at the Heide Museum of Modern Art explores how the famous Heide Circle reimagined the family in radical ways in the 50s and 60s.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair and wearing glasses, a pink top and maroon pants leans against a brick wall in a garden.

items.title: Man in serious condition after being shot by Tasmania Police

children.content: A man is in a serious but stable condition after being shot by police during an altercation in Hobart's northern suburbs early on Sunday morning.

image.altText: Two police cars parked on a street with a drone in the air

items.title: Whole villages in Sumatra erased overnight — and still living in the wreckage

children.content: Months after floods killed more than a thousand people across Sumatra, residents are still digging their way out of the wreckage. While survivors are eager to rebuild, many just can’t shake the fear that the next catastrophe is just around the corner.

children.content: A remote community in the APY Lands have woken up to rattling shelves and swinging lights caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake, Geoscience Australia says.

items.title: Travellers celebrate as major highway reopens after Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Holiday makers took a gamble heading to WA's north hoping a major highway would reopen in time for Easter.

image.altText: Four men play cricket on a road with a boat and caravan parked nearby.

items.title: Walsh facing up to six weeks on sidelines with fractured cheekbone

children.content: The Brisbane Broncos are dealt a massive blow in their NRL premiership defence, with Reece Walsh set for a lengthy stint away from the game.

image.altText: A rugby league player in white holds his face and walks off

items.title: Huge sea turtle rescued after being discovered stranded upside down

children.content: A large sea turtle, estimated to be at least 30 years old and weighing more than 50 kilograms, is recovering in a wildlife sanctuary after being spotted stuck in dunes on a northern NSW beach.

gallery.altText: A large turtle on the sand at a beach.

thumbnail.altText: A large turtle on the sand at a beach.

items.title: Motorists urged to dob in service stations over fuel price hikes

children.content: The NSW government fast-tracks a compliance blitz to strengthen fuel price transparency and protect motorists from misleading pricing.

image.altText: a NSW Fair Trading officer holding a mobile phone carrying out an inspection

items.title: After NT spent 'ridiculous' millions on aircraft, operations move offshore

children.content: A modern version of a 1960s-era aircraft that can land on sea and snow was meant to be built in NSW, and then in the NT. But years later it still hasn’t taken off.

image.altText: A blue plane over the map of the Australia, and images  of Darwin City

items.title: Artemis crew enter day four with plans to take manual control of spacecraft

children.content: The Artemis astronauts have been taking a moment to look back at Earth as they continue deep into space toward the Moon.

gallery.altText: A women peers out of a spacecraft cabin window, looking down at Earth. Her long hair floats upwards in the zero gravity.

thumbnail.altText: A women peers out of a spacecraft cabin window, looking down at Earth. Her long hair floats upwards in the zero gravity.

items.title: Too young for social media, but old enough to be locked up

children.content: As a nation, we have decided that a teenager cannot navigate a social media algorithm, but a 10-year-old can navigate a courtroom. For Australia's 2025 UN youth representative Satara Uthayakumaran, the contradiction is impossible to ignore.

image.altText: A young women sits listening to young unidentified detainees in a youth detention centre

items.title: The 'mudcrabs' battling to remediate Sydney's sickest waterway

children.content: Scurrying about the banks of Sydney's Cooks River, an army of volunteers known as the 'mudcrabs' is working to bring one of the nation's most toxic waterways back to life.

image.altText: A number of men, women and young people working together to care for vegetation on the side of a path.

items.title: Albany woman smashes WA's record for heaviest pumpkin with 408kg feat

children.content: A pumpkin grown in Albany has beaten the previous WA record for heaviest pumpkin by nearly 60 kilograms.

gallery.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck

thumbnail.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck

items.title: 'Chock-a-block' cemetery means non-Catholics have no grave options

children.content: The Anglican church in a small northern Tasmanian town has exhausted all its cemetery space after selling off land to accommodate new social and affordable housing.

image.altText: A woman in a blue cardigan stands in a cemetery on a cloudy day.

items.title: 'Amazing' breakthrough as rare bird relearns its lost 'love song'

children.content: There are only about 250 regent honeyeaters left in the wild, and they have been losing their unique call, mimicking other birds instead. Conservationists say they are now successfully teaching young birds their traditional song.

image.altText: Regent honeyeater and wattle bird
